usage: In-repo CodeScene Code Health customisation (https://docs.enterprise.codescene.io/versions/6.0.34/guides/technical/code-health.html). Rule sets are evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins by matching_content_path. Per the docs, a rule weight of 0.0 DISABLES that rule and it is 'no longer part of the code health calculation', so a path whose complexity/duplication/size rules are all zero-weighted scores a perfect 10.00 and is not counted by the 'New code is healthy' gate. This file exists for ONE reason: eval/calibration-known-bad/fixtures/** is deliberately unhealthy code (it calibrates our own health gates - a gate that never fires on bad input is theatre, per the Constitution Rule 4). Those fixtures must BE unhealthy; they are not refactored and not UI-suppressed. NOTE: CodeScene has no in-repo field that skips analysis entirely; weight 0.0 is the documented in-repo mechanism that neutralises the health calculation for a path. A full analysis-exclusion glob (project 'Exclude Content') is web-UI only and may be set additionally by the founder for eval/calibration-known-bad/fixtures/**.

rule_sets.matching_content_path_doc: The seeded known-bad calibration fixtures ONLY. These files are intentionally over-complex / smelly so the health-gate, swallow-gate, one-door and domain gates can be proven to fire on the exact shapes they exist to catch. Their code health is deliberately not our code health, so every code-health rule is disabled (weight 0.0) for this path and this path alone.
Everything else in the repository uses CodeScene's default Clean Code Collective rules and thresholds unchanged. This catch-all carries no overrides.
